page: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is a memory-optimized virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 64 shared vCPUs and 64 physical cores operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include dedicated GPU hardware and relies on network-attached storage, providing a minimal 1 GB local storage allocation.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory-intensive and computational tasks. The server achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth tests, registering 7142.64 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 532.73 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores also rank in the top tier, supported by advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and vector neural network instructions. However, because the CPU allocation is shared, multi-core performance may experience variability under sustained co-tenant workloads, despite registering strong multi-threaded decompression and database operation metrics.

This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive memory footprints without the overhead of dedicated physical cores or local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, medium-scale language model inference, and memory-heavy data processing pipelines.
description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 64 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the x86_64 processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and VNNI. The server lacks dedicated GPUs and features a minimal 1 GB of network-attached local storage. Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for memory bandwidth, single-core CPU performance, and database operations, though its shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for sustained multi-core workloads. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, including large-memory database hosting, Redis caching, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM. It delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual server delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and processing capabilities for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier memory bandwidth of up to 7142.64 GB/sec for cached reads
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and VNNI
Minimal 1 GB network-attached storage with no local GPU accelerators
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and LLM inference
